BONEHEADZ
GYM

Brand & Art Direction

The reference for everything the game looks, moves and sounds like. A spooky-cute, screenprint-textured skeleton fitness RPG, built on the hand-drawn world of illustrator Cam Miller.

Every value here measured from Cam's 258-piece art library, not invented.
01 The one-liner

Skeletons who lift, drawn like a vintage screenprint.

Boneheadz turns eating well and moving more into a bone-collecting cartoon RPG. The feeling is playful, a little spooky, never grim. Every skeleton is a character you'd want on a t-shirt, and the whole thing reads like a hand-pulled poster, not a slick app.

Hand-made

Loose inked line, screenprint grain. Nothing looks vector-perfect or corporate.

Spooky-cute

Skulls and reapers, but rounded, goofy and warm. Halloween-adjacent, never horror.

Poster-bold

Flat, loud spot colours on paper. Confident shapes read from across the room.

Kind by design

Rewards effort, never punishes. The bones cheer you on. We never shame a body.

02 Logo & wordmark

The mark

A friendly front-facing skull, the hand-lettered BONEHEADZ wordmark, and the tombstone/pit device. The lettering is drawn, not typed, it should always feel like Cam's hand.

Badge lockup, skull in a ring
Hand-lettered wordmark
Skull mascot, standalone
  • ClearspaceKeep at least one skull-width of air on all sides. Never crowd the mark with UI.
  • Do notRebuild the wordmark in a system font, add gradients/bevels, re-colour the skull outside the palette, or stretch.
  • GroundPrefers a dark or paper ground. Give the cream skull a dark backing when placed on busy art.
03 The lineage

Where the look comes from

The art is by Cam Miller (@camereto). His world is retro rubber-hose mascots (think 1930s cartoon revival meets modern skate/streetwear) rendered as screenprint murals: bold spot colours, halftone stipple shading, hand lettering, banners and bursts. Boneheadz is that exact language pointed at skeletons and the gym. When in doubt, go look at his murals, not at other apps.

Screenprint halftone / stipple shading, from Cam's mural work
Signature moves carry these through
  • Rubber-hoseBean limbs, four-finger gloves, bouncy expressive faces on everything.
  • ScreenprintHalftone dot / stipple fills, slight mis-registration, paper texture.
  • LetteringDrawn display type, ribbons, banners, collegiate badges.
  • PunctuationStar-bursts, sparkles, motion lines, little clouds and hash marks.
04 Colour

Sampled from the art, never guessed

Every colour below was pulled from Cam's original 1000×1000 files by measuring the actual pixels. The system is simple: a warm ink, a bone neutral range, and a family of flat poster accents. Coral is the hero, it is literally the single most common colour across the backgrounds (~55%).

Ink & bone the constant base, ~60% of every character
Ink
#2A2D28
Every outline. A warm near-black, never pure #000.
Bone
#64605A
Skull & skeleton mid-tone.
Bone-white
#F0EDD6
Teeth, highlights. A cream, not white.
Paper
#EDE7D6
Light-ground backdrop.
Midnight
#0d0c12
App ground (dark).
Poster accents flat, loud, one at a time
Coral
#FD6857
Hero accent. Backgrounds, danger, energy.
Pink
#E992A3
Soft backdrops, secret dens.
Violet
#9B92E8
Epic rarity, magic.
Sky
#72A8F1
Cool backdrops, water.
Cyan
#62E5F1
Rare rarity, glowing eyes.
Mint
#A2E0A6
Fresh backdrops.
Gold
#E2AB36
Grillz, coins, legendary.
Yellow
#FCF35E
Sparks, energy pops.
Rose
#E3586E
Tongues, hearts, warm eyes.
Lime (app)
#A5E847
UI action accent. A punchier take on the art's greens.

Usage rule: ink + bone carry the frame; one poster accent leads each screen. Never fill a layout with three loud accents fighting. The app's lime is the interactive-action colour (buttons, "on" states); coral is the brand/emotional pop.

05 Line & form

Confident, rounded, a little wobbly

Line weight measured at ~0.5% of figure height
  • Ink colourWarm charcoal #2A2D28, not black. Softer, more analog.
  • WeightOne confident line, ~0.5% of the figure's height (about 5px on a 1000px master). Roughly even, with tiny organic swells.
  • FormsRounded and friendly. Even skulls have soft jaws and dot nostrils. No sharp horror edges.
  • HandsFour-finger rubber-hose gloves. Bean / sausage limbs.
  • ClosureShapes are closed and filled flat, then shaded. Line sits on top, unbroken.
Base skull
Cosmetic variant
FX variant
Skeleton, in motion
06 Texture

It should feel printed

The analog feel comes from two things: screenprint grain in the art, and a subtle paper/noise overlay in the app (this deck uses the same one). Surfaces get hand-inked borders, not crisp 1px hairlines. Buttons read like stickers, with a hard offset shadow instead of a soft blur.

  • App grainAn SVG feTurbulence fractal-noise tile at ~5% opacity over everything (see the faint speckle on this page).
  • PanelsHand-inked look: 2px #2A2D28 border + a 4px 5px hard drop-shadow. Radius ~16–20px.
  • ButtonsSticker style, flat fill, ink outline, offset shadow, slight squash on press.
  • NeverGlassmorphism, soft glchrome, long blurry shadows, or flat material-design elevation.
07 The bonehead

A paper-doll of stacked layers

Every character is one skeleton assembled from swappable layers drawn on the same 1000×1000 canvas, composited back-to-front by z-order. Cosmetics never redraw the body, they sit on top, so any combination reads correctly.

Body + skull + eyes + grillz + hat, composited
zSlotCode
0BackgroundBG
5Pet / companionC
10Body (skeleton)B
20–60Socks · Kicks · Undies · Pants · TopS,FW,U,P,T
70SkullSK
80–100Eyes · Grillz · MouthE,G,M
110HatH
120–130Left / right handIL,IR
150YardYD
Cosmetics are their own little characters
Kicks
Eyes
Grillz
Mouth
08 Pets & creatures

The bestiary

Companions are full cartoon monsters in the same hand: bold, expressive, one clear silhouette each. They level from your steps and fight alongside you. Rarity is shown with a coloured glow, never a border badge.

Day One Lizard
Rain cloud
Mallard
Catfish
Rarity glow
Rare
cyan glow
Epic
violet glow
Legendary
gold glow
Shiny
rainbow sparkle
09 Iconography

Three tiers, one attitude

In-house SVG

Hand-built UI glyphs (bone, coin, pit, trend, flame). Chunky, rounded, filled with palette colours and a thin ink stroke. Match the cartoon weight.

game-icons.net

System iconography (food, gear, stats) uses CC-BY game-icons: flat fill + subtle drop-shadow, no rim, semantic tints (gold/ice/slime/orange), rotten-tinted food. Replaces Apple emoji.

Accent marks

The punctuation of the brand: star-bursts, sparkles, motion lines, little clouds, hash marks, and loose energy scribbles for FX.

Hand-drawn energy / FX scribble, the loose two-colour mark used for combat and charge effects
10 Typography

BANGERS DOES THE SHOUTING

Display type is Bangers (this deck's headers), a comic-book face that echoes Cam's hand-lettering for titles, stats and buttons. Body copy is the clean system sans, kept quiet so the art and the display face carry the personality. Hero moments (splash, crate reveals) can use true hand-lettering.

  • DisplayBangers, UPPERCASE, tight leading, slight letter-spacing. Big numbers, titles, button labels.
  • BodySystem sans (-apple-system / Roboto). 15–16px, generous line-height, ~60ch measure.
  • DataMonospaced, tabular figures for stats, hex, timers, and anything that lines up in a column.
  • NeverA second decorative display face. Bangers + system sans is the whole kit.
11 Motion

Snappy, hand-drawn, restrained

Animation should feel like a flip-book, not a 3D engine. A little squash-and-bounce brings characters to life; effects punctuate, they don't fill the screen.

  • Line artTwo-keyframe, snap not crossfade. Hard-cut between drawn frames so the line stays crisp (fading blurs it into mush).
  • LifeIdle bob, press-squash on buttons, a bounce on reward. Ease-out, short (150–300ms).
  • GlowIn-range / rarity glow uses filter and box-shadow, never an animated transform on a positioned marker.
  • RespectHonour prefers-reduced-motion; effects are celebratory, never blocking.
12 UI in the wild

The "Midnight" system

In-app, the world sits on a near-black grainy ground so the poster accents and cream bones glow. Content lives in hand-inked sticker cards. Layout is calm and single-column-friendly for phones; the art does the talking.

  • GroundMidnight #0d0c12 + 5% grain. Surfaces #16151d / #1e1c26.
  • CardsInk border + offset shadow, radius 16–20px, 16px padding.
  • AccentLime for actions and "on" states; coral for brand/energy; one lead accent per screen.
  • RestraintWhitespace over decoration. No unrequested effects. Let Cam's art be the hero.
13 Voice & tone

The bones are on your side

Warm, funny, a bit gremlin. Skeleton and gym puns welcome. We celebrate effort and never moralise food or bodies, this is a hard line, not a preference.

SAY"Nice work, your bonehead just levelled up."
SAY"Logged it. The Reaper approves."
NOT"You went over your calories today." (never scold)
NOTAnything that rewards eating less or frames food as good/bad. Wellbeing guardrail is absolute.
14 The rules

Do / Don't

Do

  • Measure from the art. Pull colours from Cam's files, never eyeball them.
  • Ink, not black. Outlines are warm #2A2D28.
  • One accent leads. Let ink + bone hold the frame.
  • Keep it printed. Grain, sticker cards, hard shadows.
  • Match Cam. Rubber-hose, screenprint, hand lettering.
  • Stay kind. Reward effort; protect the wellbeing guardrail.

Don't

  • No pure black / neon-on-white. No generic SaaS palette.
  • No glassmorphism or soft material elevation.
  • No second display font, no system-font wordmark.
  • No unrequested effects or edits to Cam's source art.
  • No crossfading line art (it blurs the ink).
  • No em dashes in copy; no shame, no horror-gore.
FEED THE BONES.

Living document, v1 · 2026-07-23. Built by measuring Cam Miller's 258-piece Boneheadz library (1000×1000 originals) and his mural decks, cross-referenced with the shipped app tokens. Art © Cam Miller (@camereto). Update this whenever the art or the system moves.
